cit.basis: 18% standard corporate income tax (porez na dobit); reduced 10% rate for taxpayers with annual revenue not exceeding EUR 1,000,000 (Profit Tax Act / Zakon o porezu na dobit).
cit.pillar_two_note: Croatia implemented EU Pillar Two (Directive (EU) 2022/2523) for in-scope groups (consolidated revenue >= EUR 750m), including a domestic minimum top-up tax to a 15% effective minimum. Out of scope for this SME estimator.
cit.note: Administered by the Tax Administration (Porezna uprava).

vat.note: 25% standard VAT (PDV); reduced rates of 13% (e.g. accommodation, certain foodstuffs) and 5% (e.g. bread, milk, books, medicines). EU member state: One-Stop-Shop (OSS/IOSS) available for cross-border B2C supplies.

personal_income_tax.note: Municipal-rate PIT system: since 2024 the former municipal surtax is abolished and each municipality/city sets its own lower and higher PIT rates within statutory bands (e.g. lower rate roughly 15-23.6%, higher rate roughly 25-35.4% in 2025, Zagreb at the top of the bands).

effective_date_range.basis: Target ruleset/params year; jurisdiction fiscal calendars and transitional periods may differ.

rate_threshold_assumptions: Rates and thresholds are simplified for LedgerField estimates.
The 10% rate depends on annual revenue (<= EUR 1,000,000), not profit; the caller must assess eligibility.
Pillar Two, withholding, relief, filing, and sector-specific rules may alter outcomes.
Verify against official guidance and local advisor before filing or production use.
limitations: Not legal, tax, accounting, or filing advice.
Does not encode every deduction, exemption, treaty, WHT, payroll, or filing rule.
High-stakes use requires local validation against official forms and current law.
